title: Deactivate player repository and service layer
description: Implement the DeactivatePlayer operation that safely removes a player from active duty while preserving all historical data. This is a cascading operation across multiple tables, handling future fixture cleanup, availability deletion, captain/team deactivation, and user account unlinking. Must include guard rails: refuse to deactivate if player is a team captain (must reassign first).
acceptance_criteria: Sets player_teams.is_active = FALSE for the active season
Sets captains.is_active = FALSE for the active season (day captain role only)
Returns error if player is an active Team Captain — admin must reassign first
Nulls out fixtures.day_captain_id for future fixtures where this player is day captain
Removes fixture_players rows for future fixtures only (past preserved)
Deletes player_availability_exceptions where end_date is in the future
If player has a linked user, deactivates or unlinks the user record and clears sessions
Operation is wrapped in a transaction — all or nothing
matchup_players rows are NEVER deleted (historical match data preserved)
fixture_players for past/completed fixtures are NEVER deleted
ReactivatePlayer(playerID) reverses the is_active flag (re-adding to teams is a separate step)

approach: 1. Add DeactivatePlayer(ctx, playerID, seasonID) to PlayerRepository
3. Check if player is a Team Captain for active season — if so, return error
5. Cascade deactivation across all related tables (see acceptance criteria)
6. Use scheduled_date > NOW() or fixture.status NOT IN ('Completed', 'Cancelled') to determine 'future' fixtures
10. Add ReactivatePlayer(ctx, playerID) that sets is_active = TRUE
11. Create service_player_lifecycle.go with business logic wrapper that fetches active season, validates, and calls repository
edge_cases: Player is Team Captain — must refuse and tell admin to reassign
Player is Day Captain for upcoming fixture — null out day_captain_id
Player is selected for upcoming fixture — remove from fixture_players
Player has no user account — skip user deactivation step
Player has availability exceptions spanning past and future — only delete future ones
Player has pending preferred name request — delete it
Player was already deactivated — operation should be idempotent

testing_requirements: Integration test: DeactivatePlayer sets is_active = FALSE on player
Integration test: player_teams.is_active set to FALSE for current season only
Integration test: past-season player_teams records are untouched
Integration test: captains deactivated for current season (Day role)
Integration test: returns error when player is active Team Captain
Integration test: future fixture day_captain_id nulled, past fixture untouched
Integration test: future fixture_players deleted, past fixture_players preserved
Integration test: all availability records deleted for future, preserved for past
Integration test: matchup_players NEVER deleted regardless of season
Integration test: transaction rolls back on failure — no partial state
Integration test: ReactivatePlayer sets is_active = TRUE
Integration test: deactivating already-inactive player is idempotent

context.user_story: As an admin, I need to remove a player who has left the club without losing their historical match records or breaking the points table
